panels.title: Concurrent (all limiter ids)
panels.description: `limiter_current_total` — sum of live counts across ALL limiter ids. With the worker's always-on global-stress:999999 entry, every call is counted here, so this tracks total concurrency through the limiter (NOT the cap-20 endurance stream alone — that is read SIPp-side on the endurance dashboard).

panels.title: Admits / sec
panels.description: `rate(limiter_admit_total[1m])` — admit requests received. ≈ the whole suite's call-setup rate, since every call traverses the limiter (full-chain stress).

panels.title: Rejected / sec
panels.description: `rate(limiter_rejected_total[1m])` — admits rejected (≥1 entry over cap). Driven by the cap-20 endurance stream; drops to 0 during limiter_kill/limiter_netcut (b2bua fails open).

panels.title: Live keys
panels.description: `limiter_live_keys` — live (id, window) keys held. Leak monitor: should stay bounded (≈ number of active limiter ids × active windows).

panels.title: Admit / admitted / rejected (per-s)
panels.description: admit = all requests; admitted = fully admitted; rejected = ≥1 entry over cap. admit ≈ admitted + rejected. A persistent gap of rejected/s is the cap-20 stream biting.

panels.title: Release / refresh (per-s)
panels.description: release = holds dropped on BYE/terminate; refresh = holds migrated to the current window for long calls (every LIMITER_WINDOW_SECONDS). Both should track admitted/s in steady state — the full chain on every call.

panels.title: Reject ratio
panels.description: rejected / admit — fraction of admits the cap turned away. Spikes track the cap-20 stream's over-cap pressure; → 0 during a limiter fault (fail-open admits everything with no holds).

panels.title: Leak monitor — live keys + auto-cleared/s
panels.description: live_keys (gauge) should stay bounded; auto_cleared/s = window keys removed by the TTL sweep (crash-recovery / leaked-hold cleanup). A rising live_keys with no traffic, or a steady auto_cleared/s, signals holds not being released — investigate the b2bua release path.

panels.title: Concurrent (all ids) over time
panels.description: `limiter_current_total` over time. During limiter_kill/limiter_netcut this dips (fail-open admits record no holds; existing holds age out via TTL) then refills within ~LIMITER_WINDOW_SECONDS as active calls' refresh timers re-populate the counters.
